articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- **84% of landlords are unprepared** for the Renters’ Rights Act set to take effect on May 1, raising compliance concerns.  
- **58% face a high risk of non-compliance**, highlighting significant gaps in readiness among property owners.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are prioritizing portfolio restructuring over expansion in response to slowing rental growth, as highlighted by recent ONS data.  
- The shift indicates a strategy shift towards optimizing existing assets to meet changing rental demands.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey reveals that landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the housing market.  
- The findings highlight growing concerns over affordability and accessibility for new homebuyers in the UK.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords demand the new government prioritize the private rented sector (PRS) with a formal strategy to address their needs.  
- A call for recognition highlights the PRS's crucial role in the housing market as Scotland prepares for its next administration.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- Starting in May, HMRC mandates registration for tax advisors, putting conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams at risk of penalties up to £10,000 for non-compliance.  
- Landlords must stay vigilant as new regulations could impact their operations and financial responsibilities.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council claims that licensing fees for landlords, set at £694 per property, won’t result in increased rents.
- Landlords are expected to absorb the licensing costs without passing them on to tenants.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook announces that the party will not implement rent controls, prioritizing market stability.  
- The decision reflects Labour's commitment to a balanced approach in addressing housing issues without heavy regulatory measures.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council is taking action to curb the unrestricted growth of Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs).  
- New detailed guidelines for HMO management are being developed to enforce regulations.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- Tenants are set to benefit from a week-long campaign starting Monday, aimed at educating them on leveraging their rights under the Renters Rights Act.
- The initiative highlights the importance of awareness and advocacy for tenants to navigate their rental agreements effectively.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The implementation of the Renters Rights Act has led to a notable increase in Section 21 evictions across the UK.
- Expert Paul Shamplina's predictions about the Act's consequences are coming to fruition, raising concerns among tenants.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are increasing as landlords prepare for the upcoming Renters Rights Act, according to new data from Hamptons.  
- The rise in rental prices indicates a shift in the market landscape ahead of expected regulatory changes affecting tenants and landlords.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The impending Renters Rights Act is expected to significantly impact the landscape for amateur landlords, potentially driving many out of the market.  
- With just 10 days until the law's enactment, the changes are positioned to reshape tenancy dynamics in the UK.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly venturing into property investment, showing resilience despite regulatory challenges and higher taxes.  
- This trend highlights a shift among younger generations toward building wealth through real estate, even amid a complicated market landscape.

articles.title: Section 21 – just days for landlords to act, says law firm
articles.summary: The Renters Rights Act becomes law on May 1
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords have just days to prepare as the Renters Rights Act is set to take effect on May 1.  
- The new legislation will alter eviction processes, impacting how landlords manage their rental properties.

articles.title: Landlord costs soar as Middle East crisis hits mortgage market
articles.summary: Mortgage costs have risen sharply
articles.ai_summary:
- Rising mortgage costs are putting significant financial pressure on landlords amid escalating tensions in the Middle East.  
- The crisis is triggering a surge in property-related expenses, threatening the stability of the rental market.

articles.title: Fewer landlords selling as rental stock gets smaller
articles.summary: The size of the UK rental stock is falling
articles.ai_summary:
- An increasing number of landlords are holding onto their properties, leading to a decrease in the available rental stock across the UK.  
- The current trend indicates a tightening rental market as fewer properties become available for tenants.

articles.title: Landlords unhappy with service from letting agents – new data
articles.summary: This is according to a Goodlord survey
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords express dissatisfaction with letting agents' services, as revealed by a recent Goodlord survey.
- The survey highlights growing frustrations within the rental market, prompting calls for improved agent performance.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Foxtons faces a significant 35% drop in sales revenue**, attributed to lower-than-anticipated buyer activity amid geopolitical challenges, according to CEO Guy Gittins.  
- **Market pressures intensify as new buyer engagement falters**, highlighting the impact of external factors on the UK property sector.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Inflation is climbing, primarily driven by surges in fuel and transport costs due to the ongoing Middle East conflict.  
- Experts warn that these rising prices are likely to affect the broader economy in the near future.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Market Limitation**: Nine out of ten newly built homes won't be listed on the open sales market, according to Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, CEO of Alto.  
- **Industry Insight**: This raises concerns about market accessibility and transparency in the property sector.

articles.title: Tenant scams on the rise, warns Essex and Suffolk agent
articles.summary:
Paul Buck, of Essex and Suffolk estate and lettings agent Boydens, says fraudsters use sophisticated scams to target tenants.

articles.ai_summary:

articles.title: Britain’s ‘broken’ housing market at a standstill
articles.summary:
Homeowners say moving has become too costly, slow and stressful, according to HomeOwners Alliance CEO Paula Higgins.
The post Britain’s ‘broken’ housing market at a standstill appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:

- The current environment has rendered Britain’s housing market 'broken,' with many feeling trapped in their homes.

articles.title: Shadow Housing Secretary served with eviction notice
articles.summary:
Sir James Cleverly tells The Neg he blames his eviction on the Renters’ Rights Act which is hurting the very people it was supposed to help.
The post Shadow Housing Secretary served with eviction notice appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Shadow Housing Secretary Sir James Cleverly faces eviction, attributing it to the negative impact of the Renters’ Rights Act.  
- Cleverly claims the legislation is ultimately harming the people it aimed to protect.

articles.title: Landlords caught advertising for ‘Muslim only’ tenants
articles.summary:
An investigation by the Daily Telegraph found ads for rentals in London for Muslims or Hindus only.

articles.ai_summary:

- The Daily Telegraph's findings highlight a concerning trend of discriminatory rental practices in the UK.

articles.title: Rightmove launches new ‘equity tracker’ tool

The portal's new service helps to inform home move decisions, says Rightmove's Head of Mortgages, Matt Smith.
